# jamfprotect_exception_sets (Data Source)

Retrieves a list of all exception sets in Jamf Protect. Exception sets define exceptions to analytics and can be associated with plans.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "jamfprotect_exception_sets" "example" {}

# Output all exception sets
output "all_exception_sets" {
  value = data.jamfprotect_exception_sets.example.exception_sets
}

# Filter for custom (non-managed) exception sets
output "custom_exception_sets" {
  value = [
    for set in data.jamfprotect_exception_sets.example.exception_sets :
    set if !set.managed
  ]
}

# Find a specific exception set by name
output "my_exception_set" {
  value = [
    for set in data.jamfprotect_exception_sets.example.exception_sets :
    set if set.name == "My Custom Exceptions"
  ][0]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `exception_sets` (Attributes List) The list of exception sets. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--exception_sets))

<a id="nestedatt--exception_sets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `exception_sets`

Read-Only:

- `managed` (Boolean) Whether this is a Jamf-managed exception set.
- `name` (String) The name of the exception set.
- `uuid` (String) The unique identifier of the exception set.
